Revitalizing Communities,
One Block at a Time

We're transforming blighted neighborhoods into thriving communities through home rehabilitation, park development, entertainment industry advancement, and documentary storytelling.

15+ Properties
$680K Investment
21 Housing Units

Our Mission

How do you revitalize a neighborhood? You start with one block, one home, one family at a time.

Bringing Back the Block is a community development initiative and documentary series that takes on the process of buying and fixing up homes, turning vacant lots into parks, starting businesses, rehabbing neighborhood schools, and creating inclusive housing communities.

We believe Detroit's neighborhoods deserve investment, attention, and love. Our approach combines real estate development with storytelling, bringing celebrity investors, local contractors, and community members together to create lasting change.

Bringing Back the Block is proud to partner with US-Adesu and the Empowerment Path Foundation to amplify our impact and create sustainable community development across the diaspora.

What We're Building

🏠

Home Rehabilitation

Purchasing and renovating blighted properties to create quality affordable housing for Detroit families. Starting with 15 properties on Rosemary Street.

🌳

Park Development

Transforming vacant lots into community parks with playgrounds, sports courts, dog areas, food truck parking, and gathering spaces.

🎭

Entertainment Industry

Advancing careers in film, television, and media through the US-Adesu partnership, connecting talent across the diaspora with opportunities in the global entertainment industry.

🎬

Documentary Series

Capturing the entire journey on camera to inspire other communities, attract investors, and show what's possible when people come together.

Community Projects

Building partnerships to transform Detroit neighborhoods through collaborative community development.

Flagship

Rosemary Park

Transforming vacant land at Conner & Rosemary into a vibrant community park featuring sports courts, playgrounds, a gated dog area, food truck parking, sculptures, and space for outdoor movies and community gatherings.

Detroit, MI Community Park
Partnership

US-Adesu Partnership

Collaborating with US-Adesu to bridge cultural connections between the United States and Ghana through media, entertainment, and community development initiatives that create lasting impact.

Cultural Exchange Media & Development
Partnership

Empowerment Path Foundation

Partnering with the Empowerment Path Foundation to create pathways to economic opportunity through education, housing assistance, and workforce development programs for Detroit families.

Education Workforce Development

The Documentary Series

Think Netflix's "Trigger Warning with Killer Mike" meets HGTV's "Fixer Upper" with the drama and comedy from "Real Housewives of Atlanta."

Each episode features celebrity investors, contractor drama, and life-changing moments as we transform Detroit's neighborhoods. The series doesn't just document change—it funds it.

"This is a great combination between entertainment and actual community work."
